title: Coney Island

infobox.key: Climate data for Coney Island, Brooklyn

infobox.value: Jan | Feb | Mar | Apr | May | Jun | Jul | Aug | Sep | Oct | Nov | Dec | Year

is a neighborhood and entertainment area in the southwestern section of the [New York City borough](/wiki/Boroughs_of_New_York_City "Boroughs of New York City") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"). The neighborhood is bounded by [Brighton Beach](/wiki/Brighton_Beach "Brighton Beach") to its east, [Lower New York Bay](/wiki/Lower_New_York_Bay "Lower New York Bay") to the south and west, and [Gravesend](/wiki/Gravesend,_Brooklyn "Gravesend, Brooklyn") to the north and includes the subsection of [Sea Gate](/wiki/Sea_Gate,_Brooklyn "Sea Gate, Brooklyn") on its west. More broadly, the **Coney Island peninsula** consists of Coney Island proper, [Sea Gate](/wiki/Sea_Gate,_Brooklyn "Sea Gate, Brooklyn"), [Brighton Beach](/wiki/Brighton_Beach "Brighton Beach"), and [Manhattan Beach](/wiki/Manhattan_Beach,_Brooklyn "Manhattan Beach, Brooklyn"). This was formerly the westernmost of the [Outer Barrier](/wiki/Outer_Barrier "Outer Barrier") islands on the southern shore of [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), but in the early 20th century it became connected to the rest of Long Island by [land fill](/wiki/Land_reclamation "Land reclamation").

**Coney Island** is a neighborhood and entertainment area in the southwestern section of the [New York City borough](/wiki/Boroughs_of_New_York_City "Boroughs of New York City") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"). The neighborhood is bounded by [Brighton Beach](/wiki/Brighton_Beach "Brighton Beach") to its east, [Lower New York Bay](/wiki/Lower_New_York_Bay "Lower New York Bay") to the south and west, and [Gravesend](/wiki/Gravesend,_Brooklyn "Gravesend, Brooklyn") to the north and includes the subsection of [Sea Gate](/wiki/Sea_Gate,_Brooklyn "Sea Gate, Brooklyn") on its west. More broadly, the **Coney Island peninsula** consists of Coney Island proper, [Sea Gate](/wiki/Sea_Gate,_Brooklyn "Sea Gate, Brooklyn"), [Brighton Beach](/wiki/Brighton_Beach "Brighton Beach"), and [Manhattan Beach](/wiki/Manhattan_Beach,_Brooklyn "Manhattan Beach, Brooklyn"). This was formerly the westernmost of the [Outer Barrier](/wiki/Outer_Barrier "Outer Barrier") islands on the southern shore of [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"), but in the early 20th century it became connected to the rest of Long Island by [land fill](/wiki/Land_reclamation "Land reclamation").

The origin of Coney Island's name is disputed, but the area was originally part of the colonial town of Gravesend. By the mid-19th century it had become a [seaside resort](/wiki/Seaside_resort "Seaside resort"), and by the late 19th century, [amusement parks](/wiki/Amusement_park "Amusement park") had also been built at the location. The attractions reached a historical peak during the first half of the 20th century. However, they declined in popularity after [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") and, following years of neglect, several structures were torn down. Various redevelopment projects were proposed for Coney Island in the 1970s through the 2000s, though most of these were not carried out.[[3]](#cite_note-Denson_2011-3) The area was revitalized with the opening of the venue now known as [Maimonides Park](/wiki/Maimonides_Park "Maimonides Park") in 2001 and several amusement rides starting in the 2010s.

Coney Island had around 32,000 residents as of the [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"). The neighborhood is ethnically diverse, and the neighborhood's poverty rate of 27% is slightly higher than that of the city as a whole.

Coney Island is part of [Brooklyn Community District 13](/wiki/Brooklyn_Community_Board_13 "Brooklyn Community Board 13"), and its primary [ZIP Code](/wiki/ZIP_Code "ZIP Code") is 11224.[[2]](#cite_note-NYCPlanning-2) It is patrolled by the 60th Precinct of the [New York City Police Department](/wiki/New_York_City_Police_Department "New York City Police Department").[[4]](#cite_note-NYPD_60th_Precinct-4) Fire services are provided by the [New York City Fire Department](/wiki/New_York_City_Fire_Department "New York City Fire Department")'s Engine 245/Ladder 161/Battalion 43 and Engine 318/Ladder 166.[[5]](#cite_note-fdny-locs-5) Politically, Coney Island is represented by the [New York City Council](/wiki/New_York_City_Council "New York City Council")'s 47th District. The area is well served by the [New York City Subway](/wiki/New_York_City_Subway "New York City Subway") and local bus routes, and contains several public elementary and middle schools.

## Geography and climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Coney_Island&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography and climate")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/18/ConeyIslandAerial.jpg/250px-ConeyIslandAerial.jpg)](/wiki/File:ConeyIslandAerial.jpg)

Aerial view of the Coney Island neighborhood, which occupies the western end of the Coney Island peninsula

*[The Encyclopedia of New York City](/wiki/The_Encyclopedia_of_New_York_City "The Encyclopedia of New York City")* considers the area west of Ocean Parkway (including Sea Gate and [Nortons Point Light](/wiki/Nortons_Point_Light "Nortons Point Light")) to be part of the Coney Island neighborhood.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The neighborhood is situated on the western portion of the Coney Island [peninsula](/wiki/Peninsula "Peninsula"), located on the western end of [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island") lying to the west of the [Outer Barrier](/wiki/Outer_Barrier "Outer Barrier") islands along Long Island's southern shore. The peninsula is about 4 miles (6.4 km) long and 0.5 miles (0.80 km) wide. It extends into [Lower New York Bay](/wiki/Lower_New_York_Bay "Lower New York Bay") with [Sheepshead Bay](/wiki/Sheepshead_Bay "Sheepshead Bay") to its northeast, [Gravesend Bay](/wiki/Gravesend_Bay "Gravesend Bay") and [Coney Island Creek](/wiki/Coney_Island_Creek "Coney Island Creek") to its northwest, and the main part of Brooklyn to its north. At its highest it is 7 feet (2.1 m) above sea level. Coney Island was formerly an actual island, separated from greater Brooklyn by Coney Island Creek, and was the westernmost of the Outer Barrier islands. A large section of the creek was filled in the 1920s and 1930s, turning the island into a peninsula.[[7]](#cite_note-Kadinsky2016-7): 200

The perimeter of Coney Island features manmade structures designed to maintain its current shape. The beaches are currently not a natural feature; the sand that is naturally supposed to replenish Coney Island is cut off by the [jetty](/wiki/Jetty "Jetty") at [Breezy Point, Queens](/wiki/Breezy_Point,_Queens "Breezy Point, Queens").[[8]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-8)[[9]](#cite_note-NYC.gov-ResilientNY-Ch17-9): 337 Sand has been redeposited on the beaches via [beach nourishment](/wiki/Beach_nourishment "Beach nourishment") since the construction of [Riegelmann Boardwalk](/wiki/Riegelmann_Boardwalk "Riegelmann Boardwalk") in 1922–1923,[[10]](#cite_note-10) and is held in place by around two dozen [groynes](/wiki/Groyne "Groyne"). A large sand-replenishing project along Coney Island and Brighton Beach took place in the 1990s.[[9]](#cite_note-NYC.gov-ResilientNY-Ch17-9): 337 Sheepshead Bay at the peninsula's northeast corner is, for the most part, enclosed in [bulkheads](/wiki/Bulkhead_(barrier) "Bulkhead (barrier)").[[9]](#cite_note-NYC.gov-ResilientNY-Ch17-9) Two major parks, [Kaiser Park](/wiki/Kaiser_Park "Kaiser Park") and [Coney Island Creek Park](/wiki/Coney_Island_Creek_Park "Coney Island Creek Park"), are located on the northwest side of the peninsula along Coney Island Creek.[[11]](#cite_note-NYC_Parks_Kaiser-11)[[12]](#cite_note-NYC_Parks_CIC-12) A 2023 study found that Coney Island was sinking at a rate of about 2.6 ± 0.8 millimeters (0.102 ± 0.031 in) per year, making it among the fastest-sinking locations in New York City;[[13]](#cite_note-Delaney_2024_f435-13) this is mainly because parts of the neighborhood were created by [land reclamation](/wiki/Land_reclamation "Land reclamation").[[13]](#cite_note-Delaney_2024_f435-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Coney Island has a [humid subtropical climate](/wiki/Humid_subtropical_climate "Humid subtropical climate") (*Cfa*) and the [hardiness zone](/wiki/Hardiness_zone "Hardiness zone") is 7b.[[15]](#cite_note-plantmaps-15)
